questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The Government should consider the possibility of increasing the software budget from the current Rs 20 crore to Rs 30 crore in the Eighth Plan, provided there are concrete suggestions for the utilisation of the funds.

choices.I: The Government should consult the trade unions in this regard.
choices.II: Software companies should submit details and provide to the Government.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Most of the development work on paper only, while in the field work appears to be at a slow pace regularly.

choices.I: The fieldwork should be instructed to supervise the field-work more carefully.
choices.II: The supply of paper to such departments should be stopped.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Some serious blunders were detected in the Accounts Section of a factory.

choices.I: An efficient team of auditors should be appointed to check the accounts.
choices.II: A show-cause notice should be issued to all the employees involved in the irregularity.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The Government will slap legally enforceable penalties on coal companies defaulting on quality and quantity of coal supply to consumers, especially to the thermal power stations.

choices.I: The requirement of coal for thermal power stations should be assessed realistically.
choices.II: The coal companies should introduce welfare measures for their employees.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The officer incharge of a company had a hunch that some money was missing from the safe.

choices.I: He should get it recounted with the help of the staff and check it with the balances.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Doordarshan is concerned about the quality of its programmes particularly in view of the stiff competition it is facing from Star and other satellite TV channels and is contemplating various measures to attract talent for its programmes.

choices.I: In an effort to attract talent, Doordarshan has decided to revise its fee structure for the artists.
choices.II: The fee structure should not be revised until other electronic media also revise it.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Youngsters are often found staring at obscene posters. Statement I: Children should be punished and penalised if they are found doing so. Statement: In spite of the factory's repeated warnings, a child was caught Any display of such materials should be banned.

choices.I: The child should be taken away from the child and he should be threatened not to do it again.
choices.II: The child should be severely punished for his wrong act.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: A train was derailed near a station while moving over a bridge and fell into the river.

choices.I: The Railway authorities should clarify the reason of the accident to the Government.
choices.II: The Government should allocate funds to compensate for the destruction caused.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Most of the children in India are not able to get education because they get employed to earn livelihood in their childhood only.

choices.I: Education should be made compulsory for all children upto the age of 14.
choices.II: Employment of children below the age of 14 years should be banned.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The Central Bureau of Investigation receives the complaint of an officer taking bribes to do the duty he is supposed to.

choices.I: CBI should try to catch the officer red-handed and then take a strict action against him.
choices.II: CBI should wait for some more complaints about the officer to be sure about the matter.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Courts take too long in deciding important disputes of various departments.

choices.I: Courts should be ordered to speed up matters.
choices.II: Special powers should be granted to officers to settle disputes concerning their department.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The Committee has criticised the Institute for its failure to implement a dozen of regular programmes despite an increase in the staff strength and not drawing up a firm action plan for studies and research.

choices.I: The broad objectives of the Institute should be redefined to implement a practical action plan.
choices.II: The Institute should give a report on reasons for not having implemented the planned programmes.
